what_it_does: A deterministic, multi-language code-and-design substrate extractor that walks a repo tree, parses each file into structural facts (symbols, imports, calls, references, markers, signals), resolves edges in memory, and bulk-loads the results into a Supabase ledger via psql COPY. It also supports health checks, incremental snapshots, and regenerating a legacy code-edges.json from the ledger.
observations: Uses a registry-style EXTRACTORS list where each extractor has an id, version string, match predicate, and extraction function.
Python extraction is AST-based and deep: signatures, decorators, nesting depth, branch count, calls, imports, env vars, routes, events, exceptions, and open scheme detection.
TypeScript/JS extraction is regex-based (noted as a future AST upgrade) and detects imports, declarations, arrow functions, types, exports, API calls, and events.
SQL, systemd, shell, JSON, JSONL, Markdown, CSS, and HTML each have dedicated light extractors.
Edge resolution happens in memory before load, using same-file > import > unambiguous-global precedence for calls/extends and module-to-file for imports.
Files that are sensitive, binary, log, oversized, or minified are recorded as excluded or shallow with a reason, preserving coverage honesty.
Bulk load uses CSV staging files and a single psql BEGIN/COMMIT script with ON_ERROR_STOP so a failure rolls back entirely.
Health check hard-fails on parse errors and systemic empty extractor rates, warns on isolated empty files.
standouts: Open registry-row detection: any module-level UPPERCASE dict assignment is treated as a registry row, not a hardcoded sentinel list.
Open scheme detection via regex, with a registered flag derived from contracts.address.SCHEMES.
In-memory edge resolution produces traversable to_resolved values without a database round-trip.
emit_legacy regenerates the existing design/_system/code-edges.json consumer format directly from the ledger.
Incremental mode diffs source hashes against the latest run and writes a full accumulating snapshot when changes exist.
conventions: Follows the project's address convention code://<project>/<path>::<symbol> and keeps a stem_id alias for legacy graph joins.
Reuses code_archaeology.enumerate_files and exclusion gates rather than reimplementing the tree walk.
Uses type hints and docstrings; module-level constants are UPPERCASE.
Configuration is env-overridable via COMPANY_LEDGER_PG* variables and COMPANY_SESSION_ID.
Every extractor stamps extractor, extractor_version, produced_by_session, and pass fields for traceability.
Deterministic facts are separated from interpretive/model fields, which are explicitly left for a later pass.
concerns: TypeScript/JS extraction is regex-based and will miss nested or multiline constructs; the file itself flags this as a future AST upgrade.
Edge resolution relies on simple name matching and can mis-resolve overloaded or ambiguous symbol names.
Bulk load uses direct superuser psql credentials; acceptable for a build tool but a different trust boundary from runtime RLS principals.
health_check builds SQL with f-string interpolation of project/purpose CLI arguments; internal-only but not strictly parameterized.
notes: The grown deterministic ledger builder: it turns a whole repo into versioned, queryable file/symbol/edge records and loads them into Supabase, while keeping model interpretation out of this layer.
questions: What is the exact ledger schema that entry/symbol/edge CSVs are loaded into?
How does the later Opus interpretive layer read these deterministic rows and fill model fields?
What schemes are registered in contracts.address.SCHEMES and how is that module kept in sync with new address types?
Are there acceptance tests that verify the health gate thresholds and the emitted code-edges.json shape?
How does the tool handle repositories where the scan root differs from the Python package root?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches closely: the stated purpose is a deterministic whole-tree extractor and loader, and the implementation does exactly that across multiple languages with resolution, loading, and quality gates.
apparent_themes: Honest coverage: excluded files are recorded, not silently dropped
Whole-tree structural graph over per-file silos
Incremental snapshots with explicit change reporting
intention_signals: The file is trying to mature an earlier experiment into a production-grade structural indexer that the rest of the system can query as the canonical map of code and design substrates.
novelty: The combination of open registry-row detection, open scheme registration checking, in-memory edge resolution with published precedence rules, and a built-in health gate that can fail a load on extraction quality.
contribution: Contributes a deterministic, multi-language extraction and bulk-loading pipeline that populates the ledger with files, symbols, and resolved edges, enabling downstream analysis, graph traversal, and regeneration of legacy design outputs.
summary_for_embedding: A multi-language deterministic code-structure extractor that parses Python AST and regex-extracts other languages to produce file, symbol, and edge records for a Supabase ledger, then loads them via CSV COPY and resolves references in memory.
intention_for_embedding: To grow the initial code-archaeology experiments into a production-grade, whole-tree ledger builder that separates deterministic structural facts from later interpretive model layers.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO string.
symbols.description: A small helper used to stamp timestamps; it calls datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().

symbols.does: Reads a file as text while applying exclusion gates.
symbols.description: Mirrors code_archaeology.parse_file rules, returning source and raw bytes, or None and a reason when the file should be skipped.

symbols.does: Safely converts an AST node back to source text.
symbols.description: Wraps ast.unparse in a try/except so failures return an empty string rather than crashing extraction.

symbols.does: Extracts decorator expressions from an AST node as strings.
symbols.description: Uses _unparse on decorator_list to produce human-readable decorator signatures.

symbols.does: Builds a structured parameter/return signature dict for a function AST node.
symbols.description: Walks posonlyargs, args, vararg, kwonlyargs, and kwarg, preserving annotations, defaults, and kinds.

symbols.does: Appends one parameter record to the signature list.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside _signature that normalizes argument metadata including name, annotation, default, and kind.

symbols.does: Computes maximum nesting depth and branch count over an AST body.
symbols.description: A NodeVisitor that increments depth for control-flow and definition nodes, producing structural complexity signals.

symbols.does: Initializes depth counters to zero.
symbols.description: Sets maxd, cur, and branches attributes before traversal.

symbols.does: Recursively visits nodes while tracking nesting depth and branches.
symbols.description: Recognizes nesting constructs and branch types, updates counters, and delegates to super.

symbols.does: Collects raw call targets inside an AST subtree.
symbols.description: Walks Call nodes, returning 'name' or 'obj.attr' strings with line numbers for edge extraction.

symbols.does: Extracts deep deterministic facts from a Python source file.
symbols.description: Parses the AST, collects imports, module-level constants and registry dicts, env vars, routes, events, exceptions, schemes, markers, and per-symbol records with edges.

symbols.does: Builds the full code:// project/path::symbol identifier.
symbols.description: Nested helper that prefixes the symbol qualified name with the project and relative path.

symbols.does: Recursively discovers function and class symbols and their call/extends edges.
symbols.description: Handles nested definitions inside control-flow blocks, assigns method vs function kind, and records per-symbol metadata.

symbols.does: Scans source text for TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK, stub words, and __main__ blocks.
symbols.description: Returns marker records with line and snippet text, capped at fifty entries.

symbols.does: Decides whether an import target belongs to the company project.
symbols.description: Checks the top-level module name against a known project package set or relative imports.

symbols.does: Returns the basename without extension for a relative path.
symbols.description: Used to produce the legacy stem_id alias for symbol graphs.

symbols.does: Renders a human-readable function signature string.
symbols.description: Combines parameter names, annotations, defaults, and return annotation into a single line.

symbols.does: Converts a character position into a 1-based line number.
symbols.description: Counts newline characters before the given position.

symbols.does: Finds all scheme:// strings and flags registered company schemes.
symbols.description: Uses an open regex and compares discovered schemes against contracts.address.SCHEMES.

symbols.does: Regex-based extractor for TypeScript/JavaScript/TSX/JSX files.
symbols.description: Identifies imports, function/class/type declarations, arrow functions, exports, API calls, and events, producing symbols and edges.

symbols.does: Appends a TypeScript symbol record.
symbols.description: Nested helper that normalizes code_id, stem_id, kind, signature, and export status.

symbols.does: Regex-based extractor for SQL migration and schema files.
symbols.description: Detects tables, indexes, policies, functions, triggers, extensions, and table columns, emitting table symbols and policy edges.

symbols.does: Parses systemd unit/timer files into sections and key facts.
symbols.description: Reads INI-style sections, extracts description, exec commands, wanted-by, calendar, and references targets.

symbols.does: Returns the first value for a section/key in a parsed systemd file.
symbols.description: Nested helper used to safely pull single-valued settings.

symbols.does: Extracts functions, exported env vars, and external command calls from shell scripts.
symbols.description: Uses regex to find function definitions and export assignments, and tracks invocations of known binaries.

symbols.does: Extracts shape and role metadata from JSON files without deep content scanning for large files.
symbols.description: Parses JSON, classifies config/registry/data roles, records top-level keys, and optionally scans for scheme references.

symbols.does: Extracts markdown structure: frontmatter keys, headings, links, and wikilinks.
symbols.description: Produces edges from wikilinks and local links, supporting the docs/AGENTS web.

symbols.does: Light extractor for CSS custom properties and rule count.
symbols.description: Finds CSS variables and reports whether a :root block exists.

symbols.does: Light extractor for HTML files.
symbols.description: Captures data-ui-ref attributes as edges, title, and external script references.

symbols.does: Records shape of JSONL data files.
symbols.description: Counts non-empty lines and extracts keys from the first record without storing all content.

symbols.does: Normalizes Python extraction output to the shared extra_fields shape.
symbols.description: Moves module_dicts, constants, routes, events, exceptions, and parse errors into extra_fields.

symbols.does: Guards against deep-parsing generated, minified, or oversized files.
symbols.description: Returns a reason string if raw size or max line length exceeds thresholds, otherwise None.

symbols.does: Routes a single file to the correct language extractor or shallow fallback.
symbols.description: Reads the file, checks size guards, selects an extractor from EXTRACTORS, and returns a normalized fact record.

symbols.does: Returns an environment dict with the Postgres password.
symbols.description: Injects PGPASSWORD into os.environ for psql subprocess calls.

symbols.does: Builds the common psql command-line base list.
symbols.description: Includes host, port, user, database, and ON_ERROR_STOP flag.

symbols.does: Runs a single psql SQL command and returns stripped output.
symbols.description: Raises RuntimeError on non-zero exit; used for queries and small updates.

symbols.does: Returns the current git HEAD SHA for the repo.
symbols.description: Falls back to 'unknown' if the git invocation fails.

symbols.does: JSON-serializes a value for CSV/JSONB fields.
symbols.description: Helper used when writing ledger rows.

symbols.does: Writes rows to a CSV file with minimal quoting.
symbols.description: Used to stage entry, symbol, and edge files for psql COPY.

symbols.does: Fetches a path-to-source-hash map from the latest ledger run.
symbols.description: Queries the latest_run view and entry table to support incremental diffing.

symbols.does: Computes current source hashes over the real tree.
symbols.description: Uses enumerate_files and _read, marking excluded or scratch files with an empty hash to align with ledger entries.

symbols.does: Fills to_resolved for every edge using in-memory symbol and file maps.
symbols.description: Applies precedence same-file, import, and unambiguous-global for calls/extends, module-to-file for imports, and node existence for contains.

symbols.does: Extracts the file path portion from a code:// reference.
symbols.description: Nested helper used to scope call resolution to the same file.

symbols.does: Returns the reason a path is excluded from the ledger, if any.
symbols.description: Checks EXCLUDE_PREFIXES for discovery scratch, recall archives, and the separate-project claude-ds.

symbols.does: Extracts every file under a folder or the whole tree, plus folder nodes and contains edges.
symbols.description: Builds entries, symbols, and edges, then calls resolve_edges to make the graph traversable.

symbols.does: Loads an extraction into the Supabase ledger in a single COPY-based transaction.
symbols.description: Writes CSVs for entries, symbols, and edges, inserts a run record, runs a psql BEGIN/COMMIT script, and returns the run id.

symbols.does: Regenerates design/_system/code-edges.json from the ledger.
symbols.description: Queries latest symbols and resolved edges, builds depends_on/depended_by graph keyed by stem_id, and writes the legacy JSON.

symbols.does: Ensures a symbol entry exists in the legacy edge dictionary.
symbols.description: Nested helper that initializes empty dependency sets with resolves=True.

symbols.does: Validates extraction quality of the latest ledger run.
symbols.description: Hard-fails on parse errors and systemic empty extractor rates, and warns on isolated empty files.

symbols.does: Executes a count query against the ledger and returns an integer.
symbols.description: Nested helper used by health_check to gather metrics.

symbols.does: CLI entry point for show, folder/all extract, load, health, incremental, and emit-legacy commands.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, sets global project and root, dispatches to extraction or load functions, and runs the health gate after loads.
